details.reasoning: Excellent implementation meeting all rubric requirements. The code includes: (1) export_lora_to_onnx() function with wrapper method that saves LoRA A and B matrices correctly, (2) proper parameters (output_dir, opset_version=17), (3) comprehensive metadata saved to JSON including rank, alpha, scaling, target modules and per-adapter details, (4) proper exposure via __all__ export list and model.save_pretrained_onnx method binding in patch_saving_functions(), (5) correct use of ONNX helper libraries (make_tensor_value_info, make_node, make_graph, make_model, checker, save), (6) excellent unsloth pattern adherence with @torch.inference_mode decorator, proper docstrings, error handling, progress messages, and edge case handling (disabled adapters, active adapter detection, alpha recovery). Minor: Uses onnx helper libraries rather than torch.onnx.export, but this is explicitly allowed by rubric and more appropriate for layer-specific export. Code is well-structured, thoroughly tested via onnx.checker.check_model(), and handles multiple PEFT API versions.
